promptPreview:
Извлеки ВСЕ биографические факты из статьи о Carl Rogers.

ФОКУС ЭТОГО ПРОХОДА
Персона: Carl Rogers. Это часть 1 из 2. Извлекай ВСЕ факты из этого фрагмента — включая мелкие семейные детали, конкретны
responseText:
[
  {"year": 1902, "text": "Carl Ransom Rogers was born on January 8", "category": "birth", "sphere": "family"},
  {"year": 1902, "text": "Born in Oak Park, Illinois, a suburb of Chicago", "category": "birth", "sphere": "place"},
  {"year": 1922, "text": "Traveled to Beijing, China, for an international Christian conference", "category": "move", "sphere": "place"},
  {"year": 1924, "text": "Graduated from the University of Wisconsin", "category": "education", "sphere": "education"},
  {"year": 1924, "text": "Married fellow Wisconsin student Helen Elliott", "category": "family", "sphere": "family"},
  {"year": 1924, "text": "Enrolled at Union Theological Seminary in New York City", "category": "education", "sphere": "education"},
  {"year": 1927, "text": "Obtained an M.A. from Teachers College, Columbia University", "category": "education", "sphere": "education"},
  {"year": 1927, "text": "Began internship at the Institute for Child Guidance", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1928, "text": "Completed internship at the Institute for Child Guidance", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1930, "text": "Served as director of the Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Children", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1931, "text": "Obtained a Ph.D. from Teachers College, Columbia University", "category": "education", "sphere": "education"},
  {"year": 1935, "text": "Began lecturing at the University of Rochester", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1939, "text": "Published The Clinical Treatment of the Problem Child", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1940, "text": "Became professor of clinical psychology at Ohio State University", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1941, "text": "First empirical evidence of client-centered approach published at Ohio State University", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1942, "text": "Published the book Counseling and Psychotherapy", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1945, "text": "Invited to set up a counseling center at the University of Chicago", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1945, "text": "Began tenure as professor of psychology at the University of Chicago", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1946, "text": "Co-authored Counseling with Returned Servicemen with John L. Wallen", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1947, "text": "Elected president of the American Psychological Association", "category": "award", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1951, "text": "Published the book Client-Centered Therapy", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1954, "text": "Published the book Psychotherapy and Personality Change", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1956, "text": "Received Award for Distinguished Scientific Contributions from the APA", "category": "award", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1956, "text": "Became the first president of the American Academy of Psychotherapists", "category": "award", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1957, "text": "Began teaching psychology at the University of Wisconsin, Madison", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1961, "text": "Published the book On Becoming a Person", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1961, "text": "Elected a Fellow of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences", "category": "award", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1963, "text": "Left the University of Wisconsin to join Western Behavioral Sciences Institute", "category": "move", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1968, "text": "Left WBSI to help found the Center for Studies of the Person", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1969, "text": "Published the book Freedom to Learn", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1972, "text": "Received Award for Distinguished Professional Contributions to Psychology from the APA", "category": "award", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1974, "text": "Began series of Person-Centered Approach Workshops with Natalie Rogers and others", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1977, "text": "Published the book Carl Rogers on Personal Power", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1982, "text": "Ranked as most influential psychotherapist in U.S. and Canadian survey", "category": "award", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1983, "text": "Published the book Freedom to Learn for the '80s", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1984, "text": "Concluded series of Person-Centered Approach Workshops", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1985, "text": "Participated in the Rust Peace Workshop in Rust, Austria", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1987, "text": "Suffered a fall resulting in a fractured pelvis", "category": "health", "sphere": "health"},
  {"year": 1987, "text": "Underwent a successful operation for his fractured pelvis", "category": "health", "sphere": "health"},
  {"year": 1987, "text": "Died on February 4 following a heart attack", "category": "death", "sphere": "health"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Worked with Protestants and Catholics in Belfast", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Worked with Blacks and Whites in South Africa", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Worked with people transitioning to democracy in Brazil", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Worked with health consumers and providers in the U.S.", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Conducted workshops in the Soviet Union", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Co-founded the Istituto dell'Approccio Centrato sulla Persona in Rome", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Supported the 12-Hour Political Party of the Association for Humanistic Psychology", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Supported the founding of the New World Alliance political organization", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Served on the board of the Human Ecology Fund", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Questioned the rise of McCarthyism in the 1950s", "category": "conflict", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Criticized society for its backward-looking affinities in articles", "category": "conflict", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Planned to study agriculture at University of Wisconsin", "category": "education", "sphere": "education"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Switched studies to history at University of Wisconsin", "category": "education", "sphere": "education"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Settled on religion as a major at University of Wisconsin", "category": "education", "sphere": "education"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Attended seminar Why Am I Entering the Ministry?", "category": "education", "sphere": "education"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Joined the fraternity Alpha Kappa Lambda", "category": "friends", "sphere": "friends"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Studied with psychologist Alfred Adler", "category": "education", "sphere": "education"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Collaborated with Brian Thorne in his final decade", "category": "friends", "sphere": "friends"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Collaborated with Harold Lyon on a book about teaching", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": null, "text": "Pioneered humanistic psychology movement with Abraham Maslow", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"}
]
